Centered title at top: **Sensation and Perception**

Left-aligned text below title:

**Sensation:**  Translation of external information  
                     into neural codes (light, sound, smell, etc.)

**Perception:** Intimate links to memory  
                     Recognition (or failure of recognition)

Title at top: **“Primary and secondary sensory areas”**

Left figure: lateral view of a brain with color-coded cortical regions and labels:
- **Primary sensory cortex**: dark blue vertical strip near top-middle of hemisphere
- **Secondary somatosensory area**: light blue region immediately posterior/superior to the dark blue strip
- **Primary visual cortex**: red region at posterior end of brain
- **Secondary visual areas**: pink/light red region adjacent to the primary visual cortex
- **Primary auditory cortex**: small green region on lateral lower middle side
- **Secondary auditory area**: elongated tan/light brown region just inferior/posterior to the green auditory region

Right figure: coronal brain section with two colored regions and labels:
- **Primary olfactory cortex**: small blue region in ventral/lower lateral part of the section
- **Primary gustatory cortex**: small yellow region deeper and more central-superior in the section
- Small inset brain to the upper right with a pointer indicating the gustatory location

Bottom left of video frame: small playback/control icons visible. Background otherwise plain light gray/white.

Title: “Primary and secondary sensory areas”

Upper left brain diagram, lateral view:
- Labels:
  - “Primary sensory cortex”
  - “Secondary somatosensory areas”
  - “Primary auditory cortex”
  - “Secondary auditory area”
  - “Secondary olfactory area”
  - “Primary visual cortex”
  - “Secondary visual areas”
- Colored regions:
  - Blue vertical strip near top-middle: primary sensory cortex
  - Darker blue region just posterior/superior to it: secondary somatosensory areas
  - Green curved region on lateral lower side: auditory areas, with primary auditory cortex indicated near upper part of green region and secondary auditory area near lower/posterior part
  - Small brownish region lower/anterior: secondary olfactory area
  - Red posterior occipital region: primary visual cortex
  - Pink/red adjacent posterior region: secondary visual areas

Upper right diagrams:
- Coronal brain section with labels:
  - “Primary gustatory cortex” pointing to small purple region deep/lateral in the section
  - “Primary olfactory cortex” pointing to small yellow region ventral/medial
- Small inset sagittal brain at far right with arrow pointing to gustatory cortex location

Lower section heading: “Multimodal areas”

Legend with colored squares:
- Red: “Trimodal (AV)”
- Blue: “Audiovisual”
- Green: “Visuotactile”
- Yellow-green: “Visuotactile shape”

Lower right brain diagram, lateral view:
- Labels:
  - “Premotor cortex”
  - “Inferior prefrontal cortex”
  - “Posterior parietal”
  - “Posterior superior temporal sulcus”
- Colored multimodal patches:
  - Red region in premotor cortex
  - Green region in inferior prefrontal cortex
  - Small green region lower posterior temporal/parietal area
  - Yellow-green region in posterior parietal area
  - Blue region near posterior superior temporal sulcus
  - Small red/orange region near posterior parietal area adjacent to blue/yellow-green regions

- “Secondary Visual Cortex”
- below it in smaller text: “(Prestriate, V2, Area 18)”

Yellow arrows:
- from “Retina” diagonally up-right to “Lateral Geniculate” (thick arrow)
- from “Retina” diagonally slightly up-right to “Pulvinar” (thin arrow)
- from “Retina” diagonally slightly down-right to “Superior Colliculus” (thin arrow)
- from “Retina” diagonally down-right to “Other Nuclei” (thin arrow)
- from “Lateral Geniculate” horizontally right to “Primary Visual Cortex” (thick arrow)
- from “Pulvinar” horizontally right toward cortex region, ending near center-right between the two cortex labels (thin arrow)
- from “Superior Colliculus” diagonally up-right toward the same center-right cortex region (thin arrow)
- between “Primary Visual Cortex” and “Secondary Visual Cortex”: one thick vertical arrow pointing downward from Primary to Secondary; one thick vertical arrow pointing upward from Secondary to Primary

Bottom-left edge: small video-player control icons.

Title: **Physiology: Multiple retinotopic maps in visual cortex**

Left side: three stacked panels.

Top-left panel:
- Small drawing of a macaque head labeled **Electrode** above the head.
- To the right, a small rectangular screen/field labeled **Receptive field**.
- Arrow and text between monkey and screen: **Fixation/light stimulus**.

Middle-left panel:
- Two pink boxes stacked vertically.
- Upper box: left small circular area labeled **Retinal field position**; right small vertical oval labeled **Receptive field**; arrow/text between them: **Fixation/light stimulus**.
- Lower box: large oval/circle diagram with central point and radial line; labels **Fovea** near center and **Stimulus** at right edge.

Bottom-left panel:
- Blue panel with three stacked recording rows.
- Left of rows: **Stimulus records**.
- Right of rows: **Time**.
- Right side shows spike trains; bottom row has strongest cluster of spikes.

Right side:
- Small brain drawing at top labeled **Macaque**.
- Large retinotopic map diagram below in green-outlined box.
- Folded cortical strip shape, mostly yellow with purple border/adjacent region, labeled **V1 (striate cortex)** near lower left.
- Blue arrows:
  - Upper arrow points to upper cortical region with text **Respond to peripheral space**.
  - Lower arrow points to lower cortical region with text **Respond to central space**.
- Upper surrounding field shaded pink; lower surrounding field white/gray, indicating peripheral vs central visual space representation.

Title: **Physiology: Multiple retinotopic maps in visual cortex**

Left text block, blue text:
- **iClicker: How does a physiologist know when moving from V1 to V2?**
- **a.** By noting change in architectonics.
- **b.** When receptive field mapping resets to periphery
- **c.** When receptive field mapping reverses direction
- **d.** Both A) and B)
- **e.** Both A) and C)

Upper-right inset: small brain drawing labeled **Macaque**.

Main diagram below inset: flattened visual cortical map with labeled areas and arrows.
- Leftmost elongated pale yellow region labeled **V1 (striate cortex)**.
- Adjacent pink region labeled **V2**.
- Small darker pink/red regions labeled **V3** and **V3A** near upper middle.
- Lower central region labeled **V4**.
- Right-side regions labeled **V5A** and **MT**.
- Light blue surrounding/adjacent regions between some labeled areas.

Text and arrows:
- Upper arrow, pointing near upper border between maps: **Respond to peripheral space**
- Lower arrow, pointing near lower border by V1/V2: **Respond to central space**

Overall content: multiple adjacent visual cortical areas arranged retinotopically, with central and peripheral visual space represented at opposite map borders, illustrating transitions between V1, V2, and other visual areas.

Title: **Physiology: Multiple retinotopic maps in visual cortex**

Left column, top panel:
- Cartoon monkey head labeled **Electrode tip** near cortex
- Small inset of retina/field labeled **Receptive field**
- Text below monkey: **Fixation light eye stimulus**
- Pink brain/cortex schematic below with labels:
  - **Retinal field position**
  - **Cortical magnification**
  - **Fovea**
  - **Peripheral**
- Small inset on right again labeled **Receptive field**
- Arrow from larger receptive field toward label **Stimulus**

Left column, bottom panel:

- Left side: stacked cortical recording sites labeled **1**, **2**, **3**, **4**
- Right side: neuronal spike traces aligned in rows
- Caption at top: **Stimulus moves**

Upper right panel:
- Brain drawing in light pink
- Label: **Macaque**

Lower right main panel: retinotopic map schematic with green border and pink background
- Large folded cortical region outlined in dark line
- Labels:
  - **V1 (striate cortex)** along left elongated region
  - **V2** near lower left interior
  - **V3** upper central region
  - **V3A** narrow central strip
  - **V4** lower central region
  - **V5A** right-lower region
  - **MT** right-upper region
- Blue arrows indicating progression across maps
- Top text with arrow: **Respond to peripheral space**
- Bottom text with arrow: **Respond to central space**

Physiology: Multiple retinotopic maps in visual cortex

Top-left panel, two stacked diagrams plus a recording panel:

Upper diagram:
- small monkey head labeled “Electrode” above cortex
- rectangular visual display in front of monkey
- labels: “Receptive field”, “Fixation light”, “Receptive field stimulus”
- line from electrode region to visual display indicating receptive field location

Middle diagram:
- pink oval/circular visual field map with center labeled “Fovea”
- right side labeled “Periphery”
- marker at lower-right labeled “Stimulus”
- small fixation point near center-left
- same visual-field/receptive-field mapping concept

Lower panel:
- title: “Stimulus search”
- left side: four stacked boxes each with a small stimulus in different positions
- right side: corresponding neural spike traces; strongest spiking for one stimulus position, little/none for others

Top-right small brain image:
- label: “Macaque”
- lateral view of brain

Large lower-right cortical map:
- irregular folded cortical strip with colored region boundaries
- labels on areas: “V1 (striate cortex)”, “V2”, “V3”, “V3A”, “V4”, “V5A”, “MT”
- blue arrows indicating retinotopic progression
- top text: “Respond to peripheral space”
- bottom text: “Respond to central space”

General academic content conveyed:
- electrophysiological receptive-field mapping in macaque visual cortex
- multiple visual areas with retinotopic organization from central to peripheral visual field across V1, V2, V3, V3A, V4, V5A, MT

Top-center title: “Mapping visual areas in humans with fMRI”

Left: diagram of an MRI scanner, labeled “MRI scanner” with a leader line pointing to the scanner body. Inside scanner opening: side view of a person lying on their back, head inside the bore, face angled upward. Above the person’s face inside the scanner: small angled reflective surface labeled “Mirror”.

From the mirror area to the right: horizontal dashed arrow pointing to a display screen.

Right: upright rectangular screen. On the screen: central fixation-like oval/circular outline with small arrowheads indicating direction around the shape; attached/overlapping at the upper-left portion of the shape is a checkerboard wedge/sector pattern made of alternating black and white squares.

Title centered at top: “Mapping visual areas in humans with fMRI”

Lower left: grayscale 3D rendering of a human brain, lateral view, with sulci/gyri visible. Posterior and ventral-lateral cortical regions overlaid with many colored patches/bands in saturated hues: red, blue, green, cyan, magenta, yellow, and intermediate blends, forming irregular adjacent regions suggestive of retinotopic maps.

Directly below brain image: circular color wheel. Smooth radial/sector-like gradient around the circle including red at top-left/top, transitioning through blue, green, yellow, and back to red; center slightly lighter. Wheel placed within same light gray rectangular panel as brain.

Very bottom left edge: small video-player overlay icons, including a pencil/edit-like icon, magnifier/search, grid/keyboard-like icon, speaker/volume, and additional small circular controls. Background otherwise plain light gray/white with large empty space to the right.

Title at top: “Mapping visual areas in humans with fMRI”

Left side: grayscale lateral view of a human brain with colored patch overlays marking cortical regions, concentrated over posterior/lateral cortex; colors include blue, green, red, purple, cyan, yellow. Below brain: circular color wheel with continuous hue segments (red, green, blue, yellow, cyan, magenta spectrum).

Right side, above image: “Flat map to visualize sulci and gyri”

Below that: black rectangular panel containing a sagittal MRI slice of a human head facing left; visible brain folds, corpus callosum region, brainstem, nasal cavity, mouth/jaw outline, and neck structures in grayscale.

Title centered at top: “Mapping visual areas in humans with fMRI”

Left side: grayscale 3D rendering of a human cerebral hemisphere viewed laterally, with colored overlays marking cortical regions, mainly on posterior/lower cortex; colors include green, blue, purple, red, yellow, cyan. Below it, a circular color wheel with a radial gradient, divided continuously around the circle through red, magenta, blue, cyan, green, yellow, back to red. Small row of interface/playback-style icons along the very bottom left edge.

Right side, text above image: “Flat map to visualize sulci and gyri”

Below that: black rectangular panel containing two irregular, roughly symmetric white cortical flat-map shapes side by side, left and right hemispheres. Each white shape filled with many short dark curved hatch-like marks indicating sulci/gyri patterns. Small white outlines extend around the edges of both shapes. Small faint mark near bottom edge of the black panel.

Right side: dense connectivity diagram made of many small labeled rectangular boxes connected by multicolored lines/arrows. Overall shape roughly octagonal/polygonal around the central network, with additional boxes above and below.

Diagram details:
- One small box centered above the main cluster, connected downward.
- Main cluster: many horizontally oriented small rectangles arranged in several layers, labels too small to read clearly at this resolution except a few.
- Numerous crossing connection lines in different colors: red, blue, green, cyan, magenta, purple, orange, gray/black.
- Outer boundary-like polygonal paths in multiple colors surrounding the central boxes.
- Lower part: tapered/branching set of connections extending from the main cluster down to a few boxes arranged in a small lower network.
- Bottom-right small box clearly labeled “Pulv”.
- Near the lower middle, two adjacent boxes clearly labeled “MT” and “MST”.
- Bottom center below the diagram: small panel label “b”.

Background plain light gray/white. Black vertical bars on far left and far right edges of the video frame.

Title: **Cortical visual areas form two pathways**

Main diagram of visual processing pathways:
- Left top: small brain drawing labeled **Macaque**.
- Brain regions labeled on drawing:
  - **Parietal cortex**
  - **Occipital cortex**
  - **Inferotemporal cortex**
- Blue arrow from eye input toward cortex on brain drawing.

Flowchart from retina through visual areas:
- **Retina** → **LGN** → **V1**
- From **V1** to **V2**
- From **V1** to **V3**
- From **V1** downward to **V4**
- From **V2** to **MT**
- From **V3** to **MT**
- **MT** → **MST**
- **MT** → **FST**
- **MST** → **7a**
- **V4** → **TEO**
- **TEO** → **TE**
- **V4** also receives input from **V3**
- **V4** shown near lower center as a hub below yellow region
- **LP** box between dorsal and ventral streams; arrows from **MT** toward **LP** and from **LP** toward **TEO**

Background color regions behind pathway:
- light blue/gray at left for early visual stages
- yellow center region around **V1, V2, V3, V4, MT**
- green upper-right region labeled **Parietal cortex**
- pink lower-right region labeled **Inferotemporal cortex**

Right side large labels:
- **Dorsal**
- **Ventral**

Bottom blue strip labeled at right:
- **Optimal stimulus**

Three example optimal stimuli aligned beneath pathway progression:
- left: small yellow dot under early stages
- middle: vertical black-and-yellow bar pattern under intermediate stages
- right-middle: circular pinwheel-like black/yellow segmented pattern under dorsal/ventral later stages
- far right: yellow face-like circle with two eyes and a mouth under **TE**

Bottom-left inset:
- Title: **Comparison of receptive fields**
- Dashed horizontal line pointing to label: **TE receptive field**
- Solid horizontal line pointing to label: **V4 receptive field**
- Small central blue dashed square labeled **V1 receptive field**
- Larger dashed purple rectangle around fixation labeled **V4 receptive field**
- Largest dashed red rectangle labeled **TEO receptive field**
- Vertical dashed line to top label **Fixation point**

Bottom-right text:
- **Size of RFs increase**

Motion column:
- Exp: white rectangular field with many small black dots, plus several red arrows indicating motion directions
- Control: white rectangular field with many small black dots, no arrows

“V1”

- Top image (V4): activation associated with color
- Middle image (V5 (MT)): activation associated with motion
- Bottom image (V1): activation associated with both color and motion versus control

title at upper left; beneath it two stimulus columns; left margin row labels “Exp” and “Control”; right side three stacked PET images with captions and labels V4, V5 (MT), V1.

Title at top center: **“Hemiachromatopsia: Unilateral lesion”**

Left figure: rectangular photograph of a flower bed split vertically into two perceptual halves.  
- Left half in normal color: dense flowers and foliage with visible yellow, orange, red, white, and purple flowers; green stems/leaves; dark soil/background.  
- Right half desaturated/achromatic: same scene rendered in grayscale/whitish tones, including flowers and foliage, with a bright pale vertical band near the boundary between colored and grayscale regions.

Right figure: yellow square panel with line drawings of objects, indicating preserved form recognition without color.  
- Top: curved banana drawn in outline.  
- Upper right: round fruit with small stem/leaf, outlined.  
- Lower left: simple plant with stem and several leaves, outlined.  
- Lower right: halved citrus fruit; one half shown as circular cross-section with segmented interior, one half shown as a bowl-like cut fruit in perspective.

Text below right figure: **“Preserved shape knowledge (drawings)”**.  
Small cursor/mark below this text.  
Plain light gray slide background.

Top centered title, two lines:
- “Bilateral achromatopsia:”
- “Oliver Sack’s case of the color-blind painter, Mr. I.”

Left-aligned heading below:
- “Letter from patient to Sacks:”

Indented quotation text below, transcribed exactly:
- “I am a rather successful artist just past 65 years of age.
- I was driving my car and was hit by a small truck on the passenger side of my vehicle… While taking an eye examination, it was discovered that I was unable to distinguish letters or colors. My vision was such that everything appeared to me as viewing a black and white television screen.
- Within days, I could distinguish letters and my vision became that of an eagle—I can see a worm wriggling a block away. The sharpness of focus is incredible.
- BUT—I AM ABSOLUTELY COLOR BLIND.”’

Plain text slide only; white background, black serif font.

Text on slide:

“Experience of visual world can be drastically altered.
Oliver Sack’s case of the color-blind painter, Mr. I.”

“Found foods disgusting due to grayish, dead appearance
    -- closing eyes didn’t help because image remained grey”

“Humans no longer looked attractive
    -- "like animated gray statues”
    -- including his own image in mirror
    -- avoided social situations
    -- repulsed by sex; flesh looked “rat-colored"”

“Color knowledge remained extraordinary
    -- could name color of Van Gogh's billiard table
       and the walls
    -- But unable to see with eyes or imagination”

Bottom right: small reproduction of a painting of an interior room with a green billiard table at center, warm red/orange walls, hanging lamps above, chairs and figures around the room, in Van Gogh style.
